claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to know a bit about your child or children.

**First, let me ask about the child's age:**

The eligibility for Child Benefit depends on the age of the child. Could you tell me:
- **How old is your child?** (Or if you have multiple children, let's start with one and we can check each of them)

This will help me determine which eligibility path applies to your situation:
- Children under 16 typically qualify automatically (with some conditions)
- Young people aged 16-19 may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Those aged 20 or over generally don't qualify

What's your child's age?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I have one child - Alex. He's 17 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So Alex is 17 years old and lives at home with you.

Since Alex is between 16 and 19 years old, his eligibility for Child Benefit depends on whether he's in approved education or training. Let me navigate to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now I need to understand Alex's current education or training situation.

**Is Alex currently:**

1. **In approved education or training** (such as full-time non-advanced education at school, college, or an approved training program)?

2. **Has he recently left education or training** (within the last few months)?

3. **Neither** - he's not in education/training and hasn't recently left?

Could you let me know which of these applies to Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's in education - he's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Alex is studying for his A-levels at sixth form college. Let me check the education level requirements now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! A-levels are an approved (non-advanced) qualification for Child Benefit purposes. Now I need to check a few more details about Alex's education:

**1. Is Alex studying full-time?** (This means at least 12 hours per week of supervised study)

**2. Is this education funded by an employer, or does Alex have an agreed job lined up with an employer who's funding the course?**

**3. Is Alex married or in a civil partnership?** (This is relevant for 16-19 year olds)

Could you answer these questions about Alex's situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's studying full-time, yes - it's definitely more than 12 hours a week. No, it's not funded by an employer - it's just regular sixth form college. And no, he's not married.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Alex meets all the criteria for approved education:
- ✓ He's studying A-levels (approved non-advanced qualification)
- ✓ He's studying full-time (more than 12 hours per week)
- ✓ It's not employer-funded
- ✓ He's not married

Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check Alex's work situation:

**Does Alex work less than 24 hours per week (or is he not in paid work at all)?**

This is important because young people aged 16+ who work 24 or more hours per week may not qualify for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all - he's just focused on his studies.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Alex doesn't work at all, so he definitely meets this requirement (working less than 24 hours per week).

Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to check about benefits:

**Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right?** Specifically:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit
- Tax credits

Or is he NOT receiving any of these benefits? (which would mean he still qualifies)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own right, so that's another requirement met.

Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about apprenticeships:

**Is Alex doing an apprenticeship?**

This is relevant because:
- Most apprenticeships (in England and Scotland) disqualify young people from Child Benefit
- Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are approved
- Regular education like A-levels is fine

Since you mentioned he's doing A-levels at sixth form college, I assume he's NOT in an apprenticeship - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - he's not doing an apprenticeship, just his A-levels.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Alex is not in an apprenticeship, so he continues to qualify. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. To qualify for Child Benefit, you need to meet UK residency requirements.

**Do you normally live in the UK?** 

Or if not, do any of these apply to you:
- You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, studying, or have sufficient resources)
- You're a Crown servant living abroad

What's your residency situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! You normally live in the UK (Manchester), so you meet the residency requirements.

Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check if you're responsible for Alex. You mentioned earlier that Alex lives at home with you, which means you meet this requirement.

**You live with Alex, correct?** (Just confirming what you mentioned earlier)

This shows you're responsible for him, which is one of the key requirements.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! You live with Alex, so you're clearly responsible for him. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about other potential claimants:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** 

For example:
- Alex's other parent
- Another family member
- Anyone else

If there is another claimant, have you agreed with them that you should be the one to claim instead?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! There's no other claimant for Alex, so we can continue. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check Alex's living situation. You mentioned he lives at home with you, but I need to make sure there are no special circumstances:

**Which of these applies to Alex?**

1. **In hospital** - is he currently in hospital?
2. **In care** - is he in local authority care?
3. **Fostered** - is he being fostered?
4. **Normal living** - living at home with you (as you've mentioned)

I assume it's option 4 - normal living at home with you - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - he's just living normally at home with me. He's not in hospital, care, or fostered.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Alex is living normally at home with you. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Just one final check:

**Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, are you receiving any payments from the local council specifically for Alex's living costs or care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Let me confirm the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE**

You meet all the Child Benefit eligibility requirements for Alex. Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✅ **Alex (age 17)** qualifies because:
- He's studying A-levels full-time at sixth form college (approved non-advanced education)
- He's not working or working less than 24 hours per week
- He's not receiving benefits in his own right
- He's not in a disqualifying apprenticeship

✅ **You qualify as the claimant** because:
- You normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- You live with Alex and are responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may have to pay a tax charge. You can still claim but may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.
5. **Payments**: Child Benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

**Apply here:** https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Do you have any questions about applying or anything else about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and studying A-levels full-time at sixth form college (more than 12 hours per week of supervised study). A-levels are approved non-advanced education. Alex is not working (less than 24 hours per week), not receiving any benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), lives with Alex and is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex lives normally at home and the local council is not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 17-year-old son Alex who is in approved full-time education (A-levels) and living at home in Manchester.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
response.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

response.question: Is the child in approved (non-advanced) education or training?
response.help_text: Approved education: A-levels, T-levels, NVQ Level 3 or below, Scottish Highers/Advanced Highers. Advanced (NOT approved): University degrees, HNC, HND, NVQ Level 4+. Must be full-time (12+ hours/week supervised study).

criteria.must_not_be_married_unless_dependent: Married 16-19 year olds only qualify if financially dependent on claimant

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and studying A-levels full-time at sixth form college (more than 12 hours per week of supervised study). A-levels are approved non-advanced education. Alex is not working (less than 24 hours per week), not receiving any benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), lives with Alex and is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex lives normally at home and the local council is not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 17-year-old son Alex who is in approved full-time education (A-levels) and living at home in Manchester.
